prompt: Our report cache hits basically 0% in production — the warehouse fetch count tracks the request count almost 1:1 and the cache size grows without bound with what look like duplicates of the same query. I half suspected Map itself was broken, but no: two back-to-back runs of the very same report are two warehouse fetches. Worse, the /debug/cache journal now shows queries nobody ever ran — date ranges from a later request smeared over earlier journal entries. (The dashboard mutates its params object after firing a request; that shouldn't matter, right?) And a customer noticed that after they sorted the rows of a 'cached' report client-side, the next cached response came back pre-mutated. What I need from report_cache.ts: logically equal queries hit the same entry no matter who constructed the object or in what property order (nested filters too); invalidate works when handed an equal literal; the journal, stats, and cached rows are immune to caller mutation; and two genuinely different queries never share an entry however the values are spelled. Repro: `node --test test_report_cache.ts`. Tests are read-only.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: Build/import of report_cache.ts exits 0. node --test exits 1 with 3 pass / 8 fail: equal fresh literals miss the cache (fetcher called twice for one query), invalidate with an equal literal returns false, mutating the params object after a run rewrites the recorded journal entry, and rows mutated by a caller come back mutated from the cache.
